# Coding Plan for openclaw/openclaw#60237: [Feature]: Add threadPromptGuideline for customizable auto-generated Discord thread titles

Source report: [records/openclaw-openclaw/items/60237.md](records/openclaw-openclaw/items/60237.md)

## Summary

Keep open. Current main still has fixed Discord generated-title prompting with no channel config field, schema entry, resolved config plumbing, docs, or tests for custom title guidance; the related merged PR added generated titles, while later related work only adjusts timeout/token behavior.

## Plan

Implement the Discord generated auto-thread title customization requested in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/60237. Add a backward-compatible optional per-channel config field, preferably `threadPromptGuideline` unless nearby config naming clearly suggests a better name, used only when `autoThreadName === "generated"`. Keep the existing concise/title-only system constraints as the base prompt and append a trimmed, bounded operator guidance string rather than replacing all safety/format constraints. Enforce the requested 500-character maximum in the schema or normalization layer. Plumb the resolved channel config through `extensions/discord/src/monitor/allow-list.ts` and `threading.auto-thread.ts` into `generateThreadTitle`, update generated-title tests for fallback and custom guidance, add config-schema coverage, update Discord config UI hints and docs, and add a changelog entry. Do not change unrelated generated-title timeout/token behavior already covered by https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/64734.
